title: Pronephrium inaequilobatum

Research has shown that Pronephrium inaequilobatum possesses various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various diseases such as arthritis and wounds. Pronephrium inaequilobatum is commonly used in traditional medicine and is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, as well as being used in the form of a topical paste or cream.
taxonomy: Pronephrium inaequilobatum is classified within the family Marattiaceae, a group of ancient and primitive ferns. This species belongs to the genus Pronephrium, which comprises a diverse range of tropical ferns. The Marattiaceae family is one of the most ancient groups of ferns, with fossils dating back to the Paleozoic era. Its classification within the Marattiaceae family reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history.
